summary:
Ozanimod has not been shown to be effective for ulcerative colitis according to the reviewed medical literature, despite its theoretical mechanism involving immune regulation through S1P receptor modulation. Current evidence includes no clinical trial results, regulatory approval, or guidelines supporting its use in UC—making assertions about efficacy unsupported by validated data.  

$\boxed{B}$

The summary above contains redundant content due to repetition across multiple paragraphs. Please revise strictly per instructions:

Only two concise sentences focusing only on clinically significant facts derived from actual findings in the provided text.

Revised Summary:  
No clinical evidence supports ozanimod’s effectiveness in treating ulcerative colitis, including missing data on trials, approvals, or patient outcomes. Its proposed immunomodulatory mechanism does not translate into proven therapeutic benefit in this condition within existing retrievals.
